chemistry_overall: Candidates 13 and 20 show acceptable performance with minor kinetic limitations, while 16 and 19 have hard-gate mixing failures. Candidates 2 and 3 have hard-gate mixing failures and insufficient conversion. Candidates 1 and 4 are viable but need concentration increase for better kinetics. Candidates 5 and 6 have dual-criterion mixing failures requiring blocking. Candidates 7 and 8 are viable with good mixing but need longer residence times for optimal conversion. Candidates 9, 11, and 12 have hard-gate mixing failures. Only candidate 10 is viable with good conversion and manageable length.

kinetics_overall: Candidates 13 and 20 show acceptable performance with moderate conversion (~76%) but undersized residence times. Candidates 16 and 19 have severe mixing limitations and poor conversions requiring blocking. Candidates 2 and 3 have hard-gate mixing failures and insufficient conversion. Candidates 1 and 4 are viable but undersized for kinetics. Candidates 5 and 6 have dual-criterion mixing failures requiring blocking. Candidates 7 and 8 are acceptable with 7 showing excellent conversion. Multiple candidates suffer from severe mixing limitations with hard gate failures. Only candidate 10 passes hard gates but requires long tubing.

fluidics_overall: Candidates 13 and 20 show acceptable performance with minor kinetic limitations. Candidates 16 and 19 have severe mixing issues requiring diameter reduction. Candidates 2 and 3 have hard-gate mixing failures requiring diameter reduction. Candidates 1 and 4 show acceptable performance with minor kinetic undersizing. Candidates 5 and 6 have hard-gated mixing failures requiring diameter reduction. Candidates 7 and 8 show acceptable fluidics with minor optimization potential. Multiple candidates suffer from dual-criterion mixing failures with high Da_mass values. Candidate 10 shows best performance with acceptable mixing and conversion.

safety_overall: Candidates 13 and 20 are acceptable with minor kinetic undersizing. Candidates 16 and 19 have mixing failures requiring design revisions. Candidates 2 and 3 have hard gate failures for mixing and/or conversion. Candidates 1 and 4 are viable with minor kinetic undersizing. All candidates operate safely within pressure limits with adequate BPR settings. Candidates 5 and 6 have hard-gate mixing failures requiring revision. Three candidates have hard-gate mixing failures, one candidate passes all hard gates but has practical length concerns.
